text.source_text: आनन्दमयाधिकारे च ‘ सोऽकामयत बहु स्यां प्रजायेयेति ’ (तै. उ. २ । ६ । १) इति कामयितृत्वनिर्देशात् नानुमानिकमपि साङ्ख्यपरिकल्पितमचेतनं प्रधानमानन्दमयत्वेन कारणत्वेन वा अपेक्षितव्यम् । ‘ ईक्षते र्नाशब्दम् ’ (ब्र. सू. १ । १ । ५) इति निराकृतमपि प्रधानं पूर्वसूत्रोदाहृतां कामयितृत्वश्रुतिमाश्रित्य प्रसङ्गात्पुनर्निराक्रियते गतिसामान्यप्रपञ्चनाय ॥ १८ ॥
text.transliteration_iast: ānandamayādhikāre ca ‘ so'kāmayata bahu syāṃ prajāyeyeti ’ (tai. u. 2 | 6 | 1) iti kāmayitṛtvanirdeśāt nānumānikamapi sāṅkhyaparikalpitamacetanaṃ pradhānamānandamayatvena kāraṇatvena vā apekṣitavyam | ‘ īkṣate rnāśabdam ’ (bra. sū. 1 | 1 | 5) iti nirākṛtamapi pradhānaṃ pūrvasūtrodāhṛtāṃ kāmayitṛtvaśrutimāśritya prasaṅgātpunarnirākriyate gatisāmānyaprapañcanāya || 18 ||

translation.literal_translation: And in the anandamaya section too, because there is mention of willing ('He desired: may I become many, may I be born'), one should not rely even on inferentially imagined Sankhya non-conscious Pradhana as anandamaya or as cause. Though Pradhana was already rejected by 'because of seeing, not from word,' it is rejected again here on account of the willing-text cited in the previous sutra, to further elaborate the common-drift argument. (18)
translation.technical_translation: Bhashya on 1.1.18 re-applies anti-Pradhana reasoning in anandamaya context: kama/intentional willing marks conscious cause and blocks anumana-based Pradhana import.
translation.interpretive_note: Rejection is reiterated to secure consistency across all relevant passages.

lexical_notes.contextual_sense: Tracks each block inside the section-level inferential sequence

uncertainty_notes: Curated in contiguous S01 pass with stable inferential-tag schema.
A later philological pass should revisit tadyoga nuance and the heavily compressed debate text in records 1.1.069-1.1.071.
